title: caladenia hirta subsp. rosea
intro: Caladenia hirta subsp. rosea, also known as the pink-tinged hairy spider orchid, is a rare and endemic subspecies of orchid found in Western Australia. This plant has been traditionally used by the indigenous people for its medicinal properties, which include antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Caladenia hirta subsp. rosea include its potential to aid in wound healing, reduce inflammation, and boost the immune system. In herbal preparations, the plant can be used in teas, infusions, and tinctures, although its availability and use are limited due to its rarity and conservation status.
